filters.predicate: (l_shipdate >= 8766 AND l_shipdate < 9131) AND (l_discount >= 5 AND l_discount <= 7) AND l_quantity < 2400

data_structures.fact_join: selection_vector(tile-local uint16_t row offsets for survivors after shipdate+discount+quantity); no join

parallelism.threads: auto(nproc) with static contiguous 8192-row tiles per thread

indexes.purpose: precheck candidate density; bypass pruning path when all 600 blocks overlap
precheck candidate density; bypass pruning path when all 600 blocks overlap
precheck candidate density; bypass pruning path when all 600 blocks overlap

optimization_notes: Root cause: the current hot loop is still a cache-mismatched sparse-probe plan, not a true lockstep stream; after reading shipdate it conditionally jumps into 457 MiB discount, quantity, and extendedprice columns, so the working set far exceeds the 88 MiB LLC and the dependent loads become latency-bound instead of bandwidth-bound. Strategy Category B/C: switch to a cache-sized tiled scan with 8192-row tiles so the four-column tile footprint is about 224 KiB and fits in L2, stream shipdate/discount/quantity sequentially to build a tiny tile-local survivor selection vector, then late-materialize extendedprice only for those survivors while the tile slice is still cache-resident; keep the existing 600-block zone maps only as a cheap density precheck and use static contiguous tile partitioning across all cores.
